content: Autonomous curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents, powered by intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), can be adapted to solve the core open challenge in 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory: proactively selecting which new episodic memories to retain or prune based on intrinsic novelty, rather than relying on hand-coded retention rules or task-specific reward signals. This turns memory management from a passive optimization problem into an active, self-directed exploration problem.
